title: rosa × centifolia f. sancta
intro: Rosa × centifolia f. sancta, also known as the Holy Rose or Rose of Sharon, is a fragrant, flowering plant native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions, derived from the cross of Rosa gallica and Rosa moschata. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rosa × centifolia f. sancta include its ability to soothe and calm the mind and body, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation and better sleep. Rosa × centifolia f. sancta can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in perfumes, potpourri, and other fragrant products.
